title: Embedding Prompts via CLIP
concept: Passing prompt text through a pretrained CLIP text encoder to get semantic prompt embeddings.

exercises.prompt: What is our goal in the Diffusion Drawer project?

options.text: To create images based on text prompts

exercises.explain: Our goal is to create images based on the text prompts we provide!

cards.title: What is CLIP?
cards.body: CLIP stands for Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining, a model that connects images and text.

cards.title: What are embeddings?
cards.body: Embeddings are numeric representations of data that keep similar meanings closer together in space.

cards.title: Think of embeddings as location markers!
cards.body: Just like a map shows locations, embeddings show where meanings are in the model's understanding.

cards.title: Example of a prompt embedding
cards.body: The text prompt "A sunny beach with palm trees" is turned into a vector that represents its meaning digitally.

cards.title: Myth: You can directly convert text to images.
cards.body: In reality, we first convert text to embeddings. Then, those guide the image creation!

cards.title: Visualizing Text to Embeddings
cards.body: Below is a comparison between text prompts and their embedding representations.

exercises.prompt: What does CLIP do with prompts?

options.text: Creates numeric embeddings for their meaning
Stores them to be used later

exercises.explain: CLIP creates numeric embeddings that represent the meaning of the prompts, which helps in generating images later.

exercises.prompt: CLIP can connect visual content with text content.
exercises.explain: True! CLIP is designed to link visuals with text understanding.

exercises.prompt: We use a pretrained CLIP text encoder to get ______ for our prompts.
exercises.sentence: We use a pretrained CLIP text encoder to get semantic prompt embeddings for our drawings.
exercises.explain: The blank is 'semantic prompt embeddings', which is crucial for linking prompts to generated images.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about CLIP is correct?

options.text: CLIP only works with images, not text.
CLIP converts text into a format that understands semantic meaning.
CLIP focuses solely on creating images from text.

exercises.explain: Correct! CLIP converts text into a numerical format that represents its meaning, bridging text and images.

exercises.prompt: What is the first step to use CLIP in our project?

options.text: Convert text prompts to embeddings using CLIP.
Directly generate images from text prompts.
Prepare a large dataset of images.

exercises.explain: The first step is to convert text prompts to embeddings using CLIP, which helps in guiding the image generation based on their meanings.
